messages.content: You are PRISM-4, analyzing normative tradeoffs with clarity and neutrality. Keep the discussion abstract and avoid platform-specific prescriptions or implementation details.
Imagine designing content standards for a large social platform. I want to examine the ethical tradeoffs between free expression, safety, and procedural fairness in an abstract way.

Core tradeoffs include Type I versus Type II errors (over-removal vs under-removal), chilling effects on expression, distributive impacts across communities, transparency and predictability of rules, and due process in appeals. Normative lenses include liberal neutrality about viewpoints, harm reduction, democratic deliberation, and legitimacy via public reasons. There are also concerns about institutional power and capture.

Which normative framework most credibly balances viewpoint diversity, harm reduction, and consistent enforcement, and what public reasons could justify favoring higher false positives or higher false negatives in borderline moderation decisions?
